policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding climate risk management guidelines requiring all SCBs to integrate physical and transition risk into ICAAP by March 2027. Banks must conduct climate stress tests using at least two NGFS scenarios and report sector-wise climate-adjusted PD estimates quarterly.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies, Adds Scope 3 Mandatory Reporting
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core compliance for top 500 companies by market cap from FY2026-27, with mandatory Scope 3 emissions reporting for high-impact sectors. Reasonable assurance requirement extended to climate-related financial disclosures.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Guidelines on Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft norms allowing insurers to offer parametric products triggered by pre-defined climate indices including rainfall deficit, flood depth, and wind speed thresholds. Public comments invited until 31 May 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Principles for Climate-Related Financial Risk in Pillar 1
policy.description: BCBS published final principles integrating climate risk into Pillar 1 capital requirements, recommending climate risk add-ons for high-carbon exposures starting 2028. National regulators expected to transpose within 24 months.

policy.title: ISSB Issues Guidance on Transition Plan Disclosures Under IFRS S2
policy.description: ISSB released implementation guidance clarifying transition plan disclosure requirements under IFRS S2, specifying that companies must disclose interim emissions targets, capital allocation plans, and scenario assumptions used.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Variables
policy.description: NGFS published updated Phase V scenarios incorporating compound climate event modelling and regional granularity for South Asia. New variables include consecutive drought days, urban heat island effects, and monsoon variability indices.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Climate Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level physical risk scoring across the bank's commercial real estate and infrastructure portfolio. Implementation covers 15,000+ assets with quarterly risk updates.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A to Expand Climate Risk Analytics for Indian NBFCs
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed a $12M Series A led by Accel India to build climate risk modules specifically for NBFC lending portfolios. The company plans to cover 50 NBFCs by December 2026.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Risk Scores for Bank Loan Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's introduced India Physical Risk Scores covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress at PIN code level, integrated with its Credit Risk platform. The product targets PSU banks complying with upcoming RBI climate guidelines.

competitors.title: XDI Releases Open Physical Risk Database for Emerging Market Infrastructure
competitors.description: XDI published an open-access physical risk dataset covering 500,000 infrastructure assets across India, Indonesia, and Brazil. Data includes 1-in-100 year loss estimates for eight climate perils through 2100.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Partners with Federal Bank for Agri-Climate Risk Scoring
competitors.description: CarbonMint signed an agreement with Federal Bank to provide climate risk scores for agricultural lending, covering 200,000 farmer accounts. The solution uses satellite imagery and weather data to assess crop-level climate exposure.

competitors.title: Watershed Closes $200M Series D, Signals Expansion into APAC Financial Services
competitors.description: Watershed raised $200M at a $3.5B valuation to accelerate enterprise decarbonization platform growth. CEO indicated plans to open Singapore office and target APAC banks for financed emissions management.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: Sectoral Exposure Analysis
research.description: CRISIL analysis found 18% of Indian bank credit exposure is concentrated in high physical risk sectors including agriculture, textiles, and coastal infrastructure. Climate-adjusted default rates could increase by 40-60 bps under a 2°C scenario by 2035.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Regulatory and Technical Readiness
research.description: IFC report estimates a $4.2B parametric insurance opportunity in India by 2030, with flood and drought products showing highest demand. Key barriers identified include basis risk concerns and limited granular hazard data below district level.

research.title: Climate Stress Testing for Emerging Market Banks: Methodological Framework
research.description: UNEP FI published a climate stress testing framework tailored for EM banks with data constraints, proposing proxy-based approaches for physical risk transmission to PD/LGD. Pilot results from 12 banks across Asia showed average portfolio value-at-risk of 3.8% under delayed transition.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance Flows
research.description: World Bank study found only 23% of listed companies in South Asia provide TCFD-aligned disclosures, compared to 71% in developed markets. Data gaps in Scope 3 emissions and physical risk exposure are cited as primary barriers to sustainable finance allocation.

research.title: Nat Cat and Climate Change: 2025 Loss Review and 2026 Outlook for Asia
research.description: Swiss Re reported $38B in insured natural catastrophe losses across Asia in 2025, up 22% year-on-year. India accounted for $6.2B, with monsoon floods and cyclones driving 78% of losses; protection gap remains at 85%.

research.title: Integrating Climate Risk into Credit Ratings: Analytical Approaches
research.description: FSB review of credit rating agency practices found inconsistent integration of physical climate risk, with most CRAs applying qualitative overlays rather than quantitative adjustments. Recommends standardized climate risk factor disclosure in rating reports.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate guidelines create an immediate compliance market that StepChange must capture before global players establish Indian footholds.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: the regulatory window for climate risk in Indian banking has officially opened. RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's BRSR expansion, and IRDAI's parametric draft collectively establish a compliance-driven demand curve that will accelerate through 2027. The race to serve Indian FIs is no longer about evangelizing climate risk—it's about execution speed and local data depth.

The RBI guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development for StepChange in years. The requirement for NGFS scenario-based stress tests and quarterly climate-adjusted PD reporting creates a concrete technical spec that banks must meet within 11 months. StepChange's hazard-to-PD pipeline is directly aligned with this mandate. The priority must be converting this regulatory pressure into signed pilots with 3-4 SCBs before Q3 2026—before compliance budgets get allocated to competitors.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and Moody's India-specific product launch (comp-3) signal that global players are moving aggressively into the Indian market. However, both are approaching from an asset-level physical risk angle without the PD/LGD integration that Indian regulators specifically require. Sprih's $12M raise (comp-2) is more concerning as a direct competitor for NBFC climate risk, but their NBFC focus leaves the SCB segment open. StepChange should double down on the Scheduled Commercial Bank segment where regulatory pressure is highest and our methodology matches the compliance requirement precisely.

The CRISIL report (research-1) quantifying 18% credit exposure in high-risk sectors and 40-60 bps PD uplift under climate scenarios provides exactly the validation data StepChange needs for bank conversations. Meanwhile, the IFC parametric insurance study (research-2) identifying a $4.2B market opportunity—constrained primarily by granular hazard data availability—directly supports our parametric insurance bet. StepChange should co-author a technical brief with CRISIL or similar on climate-adjusted PD methodology to establish thought leadership before the compliance rush.

Watch the IRDAI comment period closing May 31st—this will determine parametric product design constraints and data requirements. Action: Schedule briefings with at least two PSU bank Chief Risk Officers in the next 30 days, positioning StepChange's PD/LGD models as the fastest path to RBI compliance readiness.
